zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth le Strange於大約1284年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾艾勒斯莫時，她的母親Maud D'Eiville35歲，父親John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokyn33歲。她於1304年7月8日與Gruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwy在威爾士結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1334年在英國威爾斯享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth le Strange于大约1284年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾艾勒斯莫时，她的母亲Maud D'Eiville35岁，父亲John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokyn33岁。她于1304年7月8日与Gruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwy在威尔士结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1334年在英国威尔斯享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth le Strange was born about 1284, in Ellesmere, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, her mother, Maud D'Eiville, was 35 and her father, John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokyn, was 33. She married Gruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwy on 8 July 1304, in Wales. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1334, in Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth le Strange est née environ 1284, à Ellesmere, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, sa mère, Maud D'Eiville, avait 35 ans et son père, John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokyn, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Gruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwy le 8 juillet 1304, à Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1334, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth le Strange um 1284 in Ellesmere, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihre Mutter Maud D'Eiville 35 und ihr Vater John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokyn 33. Sie hat am 8. Juli 1304 in Wales Gruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1334 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Wales, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth le Strange è nata il circa 1284 nel Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua madre, Maud D'Eiville, aveva 35 anni e sua padre, John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokyn, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Gruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwy il 8 luglio 1304, nel Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1334, nel Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth le Strangeが約1284年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアエレスメアで生まれた時，母Maud D'Eivilleは35歳，父John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokynは33歳であった。1304年7月8日にウェールズでGruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1334年にイギリスウェールズで51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth le Strange(이)가 대략 1284년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 엘레스머에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 어머니 Maud D'Eiville(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 아버지 John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokyn(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1304년 7월 8일 웨일스에서 Gruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1334년 영국 웨일스에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth le Strange nasceu aproximadamente 1284, em Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, sua mãe, Maud D'Eiville, tinha 35 anos e seu pai, John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokyn, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Gruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwy em 8 de julho de 1304, em Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1334, em Gales, United Kingdom, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth le Strange родилась около 1284 г. в местности эллесмир, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, её матери по имени Maud D'Eiville было 35 лет, а отцу, которого звали John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokyn, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwy 8 июля 1304 г. в местности Уэльс. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1334 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth le Strange nació aproximadamente 1284, en Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su mamá, Maud D'Eiville, tenía 35 años y su papá, John le Strange V 1st Baron Strange of Knokyn, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Gruffydd ap Madog of Glyndyfrdwy el 8 de julio de 1304, en Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1334, en Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 51 años.
